source: EU AI Act, Annex III - High-Risk AI Systems
note: Systems referred to in Article 6(2). High-risk classification applies when the AI system is used in any of these areas. Subject to the conditions in Article 6(3) — a system listed here is NOT high-risk if it does not pose a significant risk of harm to health, safety or fundamental rights.

subcategories.description: Remote biometric identification systems, excluding AI systems intended to be used for biometric verification the sole purpose of which is to confirm that a specific natural person is the person they claim to be
subcategories.exclusion: Biometric verification (one-to-one confirmation that a person is who they claim to be) is excluded
examples: AI system that identifies individuals in a crowd from CCTV footage
Facial recognition for building access that compares against a database of all employees
Airport gate system that scans faces against a watchlist

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for biometric categorisation, according to sensitive or protected attributes or characteristics based on the inference of those attributes or characteristics
examples: System that infers emotional state from facial expressions during job interviews
Tool that categorizes people by ethnicity from photos
Workplace camera system analyzing employee mood

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, or in the supply of water, gas, heating and electricity
examples: AI controlling power grid load balancing

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used to determine access to or admission to or to assign natural persons to educational and vocational training institutions at all levels
AI systems intended to be used to evaluate learning outcomes, including when those outcomes are used to steer the learning process
examples: Adaptive learning system that gates content based on AI assessment
AI that assigns final grades in a course

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for the purpose of monitoring and detecting prohibited behaviour of students during tests
examples: Eye-tracking system that flags suspicious behavior during online exams

categories.name: Employment, Workers Management and Access to Self-Employment

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for the recruitment or selection of natural persons, in particular to place targeted job advertisements, to analyse and filter job applications, and to evaluate candidates
examples: AI that screens resumes and ranks candidates
Job ad targeting based on candidate profiles
Video interview analysis tool that scores candidates

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used to make or substantially influence decisions affecting terms of work-related relationships, promotion and termination of work-related contractual relationships, to allocate tasks based on individual behaviour or personal traits or characteristics, or to monitor and evaluate the performance and behaviour of persons in such relationships
examples: Automated task assignment based on employee profiling
System that flags employees for termination risk
Productivity monitoring tool that tracks keystrokes and mouse movements
AI that recommends promotion or demotion

categories.name: Access to and Enjoyment of Essential Private Services and Public Services and Benefits

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons or establish their credit score, with the exception of AI systems used for the purpose of detecting financial fraud

fraud_detection_exclusion.note: AI systems used for the purpose of detecting financial fraud are explicitly EXCLUDED from this high-risk category. Fraud detection is NOT high-risk under Category 5a.
fraud_detection_exclusion.article_ref: Annex III, Point 5(a) — '...with the exception of AI systems used for the purpose of detecting financial fraud'
fraud_detection_exclusion.boundary_warning: If a system does BOTH credit scoring AND fraud detection, the credit scoring component is still high-risk. Only the pure fraud detection function is excluded.

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for risk assessment and pricing in relation to natural persons in the case of life and health insurance
subcategories.scope_note: Applies only to life and health insurance. Property, casualty, and auto insurance are NOT covered by this subcategory.
examples: AI that sets health insurance premiums
AI that adjusts life insurance pricing based on health data

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used to evaluate and classify emergency calls by natural persons or to dispatch or establish priority for the dispatching of emergency first response services, including by police and firefighting, medical aid and rescue helicopters, and in the triage and prioritisation of patients in emergency healthcare

AI systems intended to be used to evaluate the eligibility of natural persons for public assistance benefits and services, to grant, reduce, revoke or reclaim such benefits and services, or to allocate or manage public housing
examples: System that decides disability benefit claims

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies in support of law enforcement authorities, to assess the risk of a natural person for offending or reoffending or the risk for potential victims of criminal offences
examples: Victim risk assessment tool used by law enforcement

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, as polygraphs and similar tools
examples: Voice stress analysis for credibility assessment

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, to evaluate the reliability of evidence in the course of investigation or prosecution of criminal offences
examples: AI that assesses forensic evidence reliability

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of law enforcement authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, for predicting the occurrence or reoccurrence of an actual or potential criminal offence based on profiling of natural persons or assessing personality traits and characteristics or past criminal behaviour

categories.name: Migration, Asylum and Border Control Management

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of competent public authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, as polygraphs and similar tools to assess the risk, including a security risk, a risk of irregular migration, or a health risk, posed by a natural person who intends to enter or has entered the territory of a Member State
examples: Traveler risk profiling at border entry points

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of competent public authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, to assist competent public authorities for the examination of applications for asylum, visa and residence permits and associated complaints with regard to the eligibility of the natural persons applying for a status

AI systems intended to be used by or on behalf of competent public authorities, or by Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies, in the context of migration, asylum and border control management, for the purpose of detecting, recognising or identifying natural persons, with the exception of verification of travel documents
subcategories.exclusion: Verification of travel documents is excluded

categories.name: Administration of Justice and Democratic Processes

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative dispute resolution
examples: AI that assists judges with sentencing recommendations
Legal research tool used by courts
AI that analyzes case law to suggest rulings

subcategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for influencing the outcome of an election or referendum or the voting behaviour of natural persons in the exercise of their vote in elections or referenda. This does not include AI systems to whose output natural persons are not directly exposed, such as tools used to organise, optimise and structure political campaigns from an administrative and logistical point of view
subcategories.exclusion: AI used purely for administrative/logistical organization of political campaigns is excluded (e.g., scheduling, logistics optimization)
examples: Voter behavior prediction and influence system
System that generates personalized political messaging to influence voting
